slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 1; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Watch the physician on screen. That pause — the weight behind the eyes, the handheld device, the corridor pressing in around them — is not a personal failure. It is not a verdict on your capacity — it is a systems signal. The National Academy of Medicine has named this inflection point. Across medicine, across courses and careers, burnout is not the end of the starting line. It is the beginning of it. You are not just an employee within a healthcare system. You are a designer of it. The solution to that moment — to the complexity, the overwhelm, the gap between clinical training and systemic dysfunction — lies dormant in its most valuable asset. In you. This is not a dead end. This is where we begin.

matched_visual_references.description: Stressed physician in clinical corridor pausing under EHR complexity
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: That pause — the weight behind the eyes, the handheld device, the corridor pressing in around them — is not a personal failure.

matched_visual_references.description: You Are a Designer of the System
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: You are not just an employee within a healthcare system. You are a designer of it.

slides.duration_rationale: Anchor slide for the full module. Motion clip (5s) plays first; narration runs across the clip and into the static slide frame. Medium content density — single identity reframe, not a list. Visual detail load medium: split layout, one illustration, legible title. Word budget 80 supports ~33s spoken plus clip dwell.
slides.behavioral_intent: Establish the core identity reframe — from clinician-as-reactor to clinician-as-designer — using the emotional register of the motion clip to ground the opening conviction.
slides.isolation_target: clinician identity reframe — designer not reactor
slides.master_behavioral_intent: Establish the innovation identity and the emotional stakes of the dilemma.
slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 1; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.source_ref: extracted.md — Module learning objectives block (LO3/LO4 orientation only; instructional treatment in Part 2)
slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 3; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: By the end of Part 1, five outcomes. Define the innovation mindset and correlate your core clinical competencies to Design Thinking. Analyze the macro-economic and structural trends — burnout, healthcare consumerism, technological acceleration — that necessitate intrapreneurial physician leadership. Differentiate a superficial idea from a rigorously vetted opportunity using Sandra Lam's Intrapreneurship Formula. Evaluate systemic operational failures through root-cause analysis. And create your personal innovation baseline: a SWOT analysis and an Opportunity Radar. Five objectives — Bloom's ladder, from understand through create. Next, we'll put those objectives into motion.

matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Define the innovation mindset and correlate your core clinical competencies to Design Thinking.

matched_visual_references.description: Create a Personal Innovation Baseline card

slides.duration_rationale: Singleton learning-objectives slide. Medium content density — five discrete objectives with Bloom's verbs. Light visual load: clean numbered list, no imagery. Word budget 75 supports five-LO verbatim recitation with framing sentence and closing line.
slides.behavioral_intent: Orient learners to the full arc of Part 1 using the literal learning objective text, ensuring they can self-monitor progress through each of the five outcomes.
slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 3; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 4; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Every innovator navigates the same arc — from recognizing a gap, to engineering a response, to driving adoption at scale. This three-course series maps that arc across three waypoints. Waypoint One is where you are now: Sparking Innovation — the gap is named, the recognition is laid down right here, and the foundation begins. Course 1 delivers a tangible output — your executive one-pager: four institutional sections built from evidence and ready for clinical review. Waypoint Two is Building the Solution. Waypoint Three is Driving Impact. One arc. You are already standing at the beginning.

slides.behavioral_intent: Frame the learner's position within the full three-course journey, activating a sense of meaningful progress by confirming they are already at Waypoint One.
slides.master_behavioral_intent: Position learner at Waypoint 1 of a proven three-course arc.

slides.source_ref: extracted.md — Slide 3 narration: 'You are stepping into this exact lineage'
slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 7; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Let's turn to the physician innovator lineage — a heritage that already belongs to you. Before this course asks you to innovate, it asks you to recognize where you already stand. Physician innovation is not new — it has a lineage, and four distinct archetypes. Device innovators like Fogarty channeled surgical empathy and domain expertise — turned a clinical observation of a patient in crisis into the balloon catheter. Process innovators like Pronovost engineered a five-step checklist that cut ICU infection rates to near zero, where others saw an irreducible limitation — the raw material was already in their hands. Technology innovators like Bath filed a patent born from clinical domain expertise that changed ophthalmology. And organizational innovators redesigned entire systems from reactive sick care toward proactive health assurance. Your archetype is already inside this lineage. Moving forward, we'll examine what each archetype built.

matched_visual_references.description: Four-column row Device Process Technology Organizational with icons
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: four distinct shapes.

matched_visual_references.description: The Lineage of Medical Innovation supertitle
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: it has a lineage

slides.duration_rationale: Cluster boundary head introducing the four-archetype lineage. Medium content density — four archetypes with inventor names and brief descriptors. Medium visual load: four-column icon layout, legible text. Word budget 100 supports full four-archetype survey including specific device/process exemplars and framing with the lineage close.
slides.behavioral_intent: Activate prior clinical knowledge by connecting the learner's professional identity to a recognized lineage of physician innovators — making innovation feel inherited, not invented.
slides.master_behavioral_intent: Connect learner identity to the physician innovator lineage before introducing individual archetypes.
slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 7; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.source_ref: extracted.md — Slide 4 narration: Growth Mindset (Dweck) + Psychological Safety (Edmondson)
slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: In this section, two frameworks anchor everything that follows. Growth Mindset — Dweck's formulation: the belief that your capacity to innovate is a muscle built through effort and critique, not a fixed trait. And Psychological Safety — Edmondson's definition: a shared belief that the environment is safe for interpersonal risk-taking — sharing half-baked ideas, critiquing peers, exposing systemic flaws. A growth mindset cannot survive without psychological safety. As you progress through this clinical course, these frameworks will be applied — along with a third framework, First Principles Thinking — first: break every assumption down to its fundamental truths, then reason upward to novel solutions. That chain from concept through process to outcome is the engine this course gives you for every clinical problem you bring. Moving forward, these conditions make your innovation work possible.

matched_visual_references.description: GROWTH MINDSET DWECK dark navy box with definition
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Growth Mindset — Dweck's formulation: the belief that your capacity to innovate is a muscle built through effort and critique, not a fixed trait.

matched_visual_references.description: PSYCHOLOGICAL SAFETY EDMONDSON outlined label with definition
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Psychological Safety — Edmondson's definition: a shared belief that the environment is safe for interpersonal risk-taking

slides.duration_rationale: Cluster boundary head introducing two foundational frameworks. Heavy content density — both framework definitions must be conveyed precisely. Light visual load: clean two-column typography. Word budget 95 supports verbatim definition delivery plus the paired-dependency framing and forward application statement including the First Principles chain reference.
slides.behavioral_intent: Establish the two foundational frameworks as inseparable paired conditions — growth mindset as individual capacity, psychological safety as environmental requirement — preparing learners for the collaborative demands of the program.
slides.master_behavioral_intent: Establish Growth Mindset and Psychological Safety as the paired environmental conditions for the program's collaborative demands.
slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.source_ref: src-004: course-content/courses/tejal-APC-C1/APC Content Roadmap.jpg (operator-confirmed supplementary source)
slides.visual_description: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 12; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: This is the complete arc of the Applied Physician Competency series — three courses, across three terms. Innovation Mindset runs through all three. Product and Project Management builds across the full year. Operating as a Leader develops alongside it. Asynchronous and synchronous formats layer through each term, anchored by assessment at every stage. Every topic in Part 1 occupies a specific position on this map. Keep it in mind — this is the architecture of the full learning journey ahead of you.

matched_visual_references.description: Three-term column structure across the curriculum grid
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: three courses, across three terms.

matched_visual_references.description: Row labels Innovation Mindset Product and Project Management Operating as a Leader
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Innovation Mindset runs through all three. Product and Project Management builds across the full year. Operating as a Leader develops alongside it.

slides.duration_rationale: Singleton literal-visual — full-frame APC Content Roadmap JPG. Heavy visual load: extremely dense curriculum grid with three terms, multiple content rows, async/sync layers, assessment row, module timeline. Narration purpose is orientation, not full-read; visual silence permitted mid-segment for learner scan. Word budget 72-74 supports row-by-row Z-scan plus closing architecture statement.
slides.behavioral_intent: Provide full-series orientation so learners understand how Part 1 content fits within the larger curriculum — building the sense of a coherent, purposeful arc rather than isolated modules.
slides.script_notes: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 12; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.source_ref: extracted.md — Part 1 Summary Slide: The Gap / The Mission / The Journey / The Mindset
slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 13; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Part 1 leaves you with four things. The gap — the friction and the near-miss you see every day — is a signal, not background noise. It belongs to you. The mission — your response to that signal is what defines the next chapter of your career. The journey — every innovator walks the same arc: spark, build, drive. You are at the beginning of a proven path, not alone in uncharted wilderness. And the mindset — the question is not whether you have what it takes. It is whether you choose to use it. The frameworks are set. Part 2 opens with your first active tool — the Opportunity Diagnostic: scan the landscape, size the problem, pressure-test the gap. The diagnosis begins now.

matched_visual_references.description: Four synthesis panels The Gap The Mission The Journey The Mindset
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Part 1 leaves you with four things.

matched_visual_references.description: The Mindset panel with choose-to-use-it closing text
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: the question is not whether you have what it takes. It is whether you choose to use it.

slides.duration_rationale: Cluster head synthesizing Part 1 across four pillars. Medium content density — four conceptual pillars each with a brief interpretive sentence. Light visual load: clean 2x2 text grid. Word budget 85 supports paraphrase synthesis of all four panels with ownership framing.
slides.behavioral_intent: Synthesize the four conceptual pillars of Part 1 in a way that leaves learners with a felt sense of ownership over each — Gap, Mission, Journey, Mindset — rather than a list to be recalled.
slides.master_behavioral_intent: Leave learners with felt ownership of all four Part 1 pillars as personal assets, not abstract concepts.
slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 13; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

rows.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 7; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
rows.narration_text: Let's turn to the physician innovator lineage — a heritage that already belongs to you. Before this course asks you to innovate, it asks you to recognize where you already stand. Physician innovation is not new — it has a lineage, and four distinct archetypes. Device innovators like Fogarty channeled surgical empathy and domain expertise — turned a clinical observation of a patient in crisis into the balloon catheter. Process innovators like Pronovost engineered a five-step checklist that cut ICU infection rates to near zero, where others saw an irreducible limitation — the raw material was already in their hands. Technology innovators like Bath filed a patent born from clinical domain expertise that changed ophthalmology. And organizational innovators redesigned entire systems from reactive sick care toward proactive health assurance. Your archetype is already inside this lineage. Moving forward, we'll examine what each archetype built.

matched_visual_references.description: Four-column row Device Process Technology Organizational with icons
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: four distinct shapes.

matched_visual_references.description: The Lineage of Medical Innovation supertitle
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: it has a lineage

rows.duration_rationale: Cluster boundary head introducing the four-archetype lineage. Medium content density — four archetypes with inventor names and brief descriptors. Medium visual load: four-column icon layout, legible text. Word budget 100 supports full four-archetype survey including specific device/process exemplars and framing with the lineage close.
rows.behavioral_intent: Activate prior clinical knowledge by connecting the learner's professional identity to a recognized lineage of physician innovators — making innovation feel inherited, not invented.
rows.master_behavioral_intent: Connect learner identity to the physician innovator lineage before introducing individual archetypes.

rows.source_ref: extracted.md — Slide 4 narration: Growth Mindset (Dweck) + Psychological Safety (Edmondson)
rows.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
rows.narration_text: In this section, two frameworks anchor everything that follows. Growth Mindset — Dweck's formulation: the belief that your capacity to innovate is a muscle built through effort and critique, not a fixed trait. And Psychological Safety — Edmondson's definition: a shared belief that the environment is safe for interpersonal risk-taking — sharing half-baked ideas, critiquing peers, exposing systemic flaws. A growth mindset cannot survive without psychological safety. As you progress through this clinical course, these frameworks will be applied — along with a third framework, First Principles Thinking — first: break every assumption down to its fundamental truths, then reason upward to novel solutions. That chain from concept through process to outcome is the engine this course gives you for every clinical problem you bring. Moving forward, these conditions make your innovation work possible.

matched_visual_references.description: GROWTH MINDSET DWECK dark navy box with definition
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Growth Mindset — Dweck's formulation: the belief that your capacity to innovate is a muscle built through effort and critique, not a fixed trait.

matched_visual_references.description: PSYCHOLOGICAL SAFETY EDMONDSON outlined label with definition
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Psychological Safety — Edmondson's definition: a shared belief that the environment is safe for interpersonal risk-taking

rows.duration_rationale: Cluster boundary head introducing two foundational frameworks. Heavy content density — both framework definitions must be conveyed precisely. Light visual load: clean two-column typography. Word budget 95 supports verbatim definition delivery plus the paired-dependency framing and forward application statement including the First Principles chain reference.
rows.behavioral_intent: Establish the two foundational frameworks as inseparable paired conditions — growth mindset as individual capacity, psychological safety as environmental requirement — preparing learners for the collaborative demands of the program.
rows.master_behavioral_intent: Establish Growth Mindset and Psychological Safety as the paired environmental conditions for the program's collaborative demands.
rows.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

rows.source_ref: src-004: course-content/courses/tejal-APC-C1/APC Content Roadmap.jpg (operator-confirmed supplementary source)
rows.visual_description: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 12; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
rows.narration_text: This is the complete arc of the Applied Physician Competency series — three courses, across three terms. Innovation Mindset runs through all three. Product and Project Management builds across the full year. Operating as a Leader develops alongside it. Asynchronous and synchronous formats layer through each term, anchored by assessment at every stage. Every topic in Part 1 occupies a specific position on this map. Keep it in mind — this is the architecture of the full learning journey ahead of you.

matched_visual_references.description: Three-term column structure across the curriculum grid
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: three courses, across three terms.

matched_visual_references.description: Row labels Innovation Mindset Product and Project Management Operating as a Leader
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Innovation Mindset runs through all three. Product and Project Management builds across the full year. Operating as a Leader develops alongside it.

rows.duration_rationale: Singleton literal-visual — full-frame APC Content Roadmap JPG. Heavy visual load: extremely dense curriculum grid with three terms, multiple content rows, async/sync layers, assessment row, module timeline. Narration purpose is orientation, not full-read; visual silence permitted mid-segment for learner scan. Word budget 72-74 supports row-by-row Z-scan plus closing architecture statement.
rows.behavioral_intent: Provide full-series orientation so learners understand how Part 1 content fits within the larger curriculum — building the sense of a coherent, purposeful arc rather than isolated modules.
rows.script_notes: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 12; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

rows.source_ref: extracted.md — Part 1 Summary Slide: The Gap / The Mission / The Journey / The Mindset
rows.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 13; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
rows.narration_text: Part 1 leaves you with four things. The gap — the friction and the near-miss you see every day — is a signal, not background noise. It belongs to you. The mission — your response to that signal is what defines the next chapter of your career. The journey — every innovator walks the same arc: spark, build, drive. You are at the beginning of a proven path, not alone in uncharted wilderness. And the mindset — the question is not whether you have what it takes. It is whether you choose to use it. The frameworks are set. Part 2 opens with your first active tool — the Opportunity Diagnostic: scan the landscape, size the problem, pressure-test the gap. The diagnosis begins now.

matched_visual_references.description: Four synthesis panels The Gap The Mission The Journey The Mindset
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Part 1 leaves you with four things.

matched_visual_references.description: The Mindset panel with choose-to-use-it closing text
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: the question is not whether you have what it takes. It is whether you choose to use it.

rows.duration_rationale: Cluster head synthesizing Part 1 across four pillars. Medium content density — four conceptual pillars each with a brief interpretive sentence. Light visual load: clean 2x2 text grid. Word budget 85 supports paraphrase synthesis of all four panels with ownership framing.
rows.behavioral_intent: Synthesize the four conceptual pillars of Part 1 in a way that leaves learners with a felt sense of ownership over each — Gap, Mission, Journey, Mindset — rather than a list to be recalled.
rows.master_behavioral_intent: Leave learners with felt ownership of all four Part 1 pillars as personal assets, not abstract concepts.
